title: Isophyllum australe
taxonomy: Isophyllum australe is classified as a species of succulent plant in the family Aizoaceae. It belongs to the genus Isophyllum, which is a part of the order Caryophyllales. Isophyllum australe is native to Australia, where it grows in dry, rocky areas. The plant has thick, fleshy leaves and stems that allow it to store water, enabling it to survive in arid environments. Isophyllum australe is often used in horticulture for its unique and attractive foliage.

It is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues.
